findings.title: ADR-0006's frozen platform seam is incomplete
findings.detail: T1's plan requires ADR-0006 to define the PTY, local-transport, peer-credential, notification, process-inspection, filesystem-identity, descriptor-launch, and clock interfaces before downstream dispatch. ADR-0006 says that interface set lives in internal/platform/platform.go, but the live file only defines FilesystemIdentity, PeerCredentials, Containment, DescriptorLaunch, ProcessInspector, and Clock; there is no PTY, LocalTransport, or Notifier seam anywhere under internal/. That leaves key Linux-first boundaries prose-only, so the receipt's claim that the platform interfaces were already verified is not durable acceptance evidence and would force downstream lanes to invent architect-owned contracts.

blocking_findings.title: ADR-0006's frozen platform seam is incomplete
blocking_findings.detail: The reviewed handoff treats the platform-interface surface as already green, but internal/platform/platform.go does not actually materialize the PTY, local-transport, or notifier interfaces that ADR-0006 says are part of the frozen T1 contract.
